meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the no-portfolio trap with this expert SaaS starter kit guide. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade apps as a student.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' trap ends when you stop building tutorials and start shipping SaaS. Use an opinionated SaaS starter kit to handle authentication, database, and payments instantly. By leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect a production-ready application in hours, demonstrating technical authority to future employers immediately.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the greatest enemy of the aspiring engineer. You have the ambition, the theory, and the late-night hunger to build, yet your GitHub profile remains a graveyard of half-finished 'To-Do' apps. The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about your ability to ship functional, scalable code that solves real-world problems.

This is the turning point. We are moving beyond toy projects. Using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you will collapse months of development time into a single weekend. By integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you aren't just writing code—you are building an asset that proves your engineering maturity to the world.

steps.title: Architect the Foundation
steps.description: Initialize your project structure to ensure scalability. We avoid manual boilerplate by using a proven starter kit architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS foundation using Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, and Drizzle ORM. Include modular auth patterns and a clean directory structure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with Next.js 15 and Drizzle ORM'

steps.title: Implement Identity Infrastructure
steps.description: Authentication is the first hurdle of any SaaS. We implement secure, session-based auth to protect user data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure secure authentication flow using Auth.js with OAuth providers and email magic links, including protected route middleware.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup Auth.js middleware for protected routes'

steps.title: Data Modeling with Drizzle
steps.description: Define your schema to handle user relationships and subscription states, ensuring your database can scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Drizzle schema files for users, organizations, and subscription plans with relational mapping and indexing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate Drizzle schema for SaaS user subscriptions'

steps.title: Integrate Payment Logic
steps.description: Connect Stripe to your SaaS to handle the business logic of subscription tiers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Setup Stripe webhook handlers and checkout session endpoints for a monthly/yearly SaaS subscription model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Implement Stripe webhook handler for subscription status updates'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Move from local development to a globally available environment to showcase your work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel with environment variable validation and edge caching.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure Vercel deployment and environment validation'

pro_tips: Always wrap your database queries in a service layer to keep your UI components clean and testable.
Use feature flags to hide incomplete features so you can deploy to production continuously without breaking the user experience.
Prioritize 'Time to First Value'—ensure a user can sign up and see a dashboard in under 60 seconds.
Document your architectural decisions in a README.md file; employers value the 'why' behind your code more than the syntax.
Audit your bundle size early; a bloated SaaS starter kit is a technical debt nightmare waiting to happen.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, add a 'Case Study' section to your portfolio explaining the technical trade-offs you made during this build. Then, iterate by adding one unique feature that solves a specific user problem.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student waiting for an opportunity; you are an engineer building the future. Your portfolio is your leverage, and your code is your proof. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
